title: anthemis cretica subsp. spruneri
intro: Anthemis cretica subsp. spruneri, also known as dwarf chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis cretica plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is commonly found in the Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, soothing digestive issues, and reducing fever. The health benefits of Anthemis cretica subsp. spruneri include its ability to calm the nervous system, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps.
